summary: Reviewed `scripts/codex-smoke.js`. The change improves path resolution, but it also weakens the smoke test’s core assertion and still runs against the mutable workspace instead of an isolated snapshot.

findings.title: Smoke test can now pass even when Codex never returns a valid final `[]` payload
findings.why_it_matters: This script claims to verify that the final structured output `[]` was parsed successfully, but after downgrading the missing `agent_message` check to a warning it only enforces `result.status === 'ok'` and `result.findings.length === 0`. `parseCodexExecResult()` also returns `[]` for malformed output, missing final JSON, or non-structured agent messages, so contract regressions can be reported as PASS.
findings.fix_instructions: Make the smoke test fail unless it observes and validates the final agent message itself. Capture the last `agent_message` text from the stream, parse it as JSON, assert that it is an array, and assert that its length is exactly 0. Keep the missing-agent-message case as a hard failure, not a warning.

findings.title: Smoke test bypasses snapshot isolation and runs Codex directly against the live repo
findings.why_it_matters: The script now hardcodes `snapshotPath` to the repository root. That means the smoke run no longer exercises the read-only snapshot contract used by the hub, and if Codex behavior regresses it can operate in the mutable workspace. This reduces test fidelity and increases the chance of unintended repo modifications during a smoke run.
findings.fix_instructions: Create a temporary snapshot or copy of the repo before calling `adapter.execute()`, pass that snapshot path to the adapter, and clean it up on exit. Reuse the project’s snapshot mechanism if available so the smoke path matches production behavior.

findings.title: No automated coverage for the new pass criteria regression
findings.why_it_matters: The behavior change at lines 60-66 materially weakens the smoke test, but there is no test protecting against a false PASS when Codex emits no `agent_message`, malformed JSON, or a non-empty/invalid final payload that still normalizes to zero findings. This makes the regression easy to reintroduce silently.
findings.fix_instructions: Add tests for the smoke-test decision logic covering: no `agent_message`, malformed final agent message, valid final `[]`, and invalid arrays that normalize to zero findings. The expected result should only PASS for the valid empty array case.

fix_plan: Restore a strict success condition by validating the actual final `agent_message` payload instead of inferring success from `findings.length === 0`.
Run the smoke script against an isolated temporary snapshot rather than the mutable repo root.
Add regression tests for missing/malformed/final-payload cases so future changes cannot silently weaken the smoke test again.
